question_text: Does a formal code of conduct exist to regulate the ethics and behavior of Members of Parliament?

Are asset and liability declarations of Members of Parliament publicly accessible?
Does Parliament provide formal and systematic channels for the public to raise queries to Parliament or MPs?
Does Parliament track and publicly report on the status of above public queries raised to Parliament or MPs?

Is there post-legislative scrutiny of laws by Parliament to assess effectiveness after enactment?

Is there regular public consultation for proposals or feedback on bills during the drafting stage, prior to formal introduction in Parliament?

Is the public formally able to provide feedback on enacted laws to inform review or amendment?
Is information provided on how public feedback given post-enactment of a law, is considered?
